steps.title: Trace Laidre's reasoning
steps.content: Laidre's argument is: if the tusk served an important survival purpose, then evolution would make it widespread — yet many narwhals don't have a tusk at all. The blank must complete the prediction her logic produces.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'more narwhals would have a tusk': Laidre says if the tusk were critical, evolution would spread it broadly — so the prediction is that most narwhals would have one. This follows directly from her reasoning. ✓
  • Choice B — 'narwhals would become less shy over time': Shyness is unrelated to the tusk's importance-to-survival argument; nothing in the passage links behavior to tusk function. ✗
  • Choice C — 'fewer marine animals would also have tusks': The argument concerns narwhals only, not a cross-species comparison of tusk prevalence. ✗
  • Choice D — 'some narwhals would seek a new habitat': Habitat migration has no connection to whether a body part is evolutionarily necessary. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
